record.context_description: Term names the punishment that corresponds to sin — the divine consequence that falls on those who persist in sin, which itself becomes a form of chastisement

operations.description: New group 2449-001 for H2403I: Term names the punishment that corresponds to sin — the divine consequence that falls on those who persist in sin, which itself becomes a form of chastisement

record.context_description: Term names the inward act of perverting and committing iniquity — the moral distortion of the inner being that is confessed, punished, and turned from in repentance

operations.description: New group 2439-001 for H5753B: Term names the inward act of perverting and committing iniquity — the moral distortion of the inner being that is confessed, punished, and turned from in repentance

record.context_description: Term names iniquity as the inner-being condition of moral debt before God — the accumulated perversion of the human heart that God marks, bears, forgives, and will ultimately remember no more

operations.description: New group 182-001 for H5771G: Term names iniquity as the inner-being condition of moral debt before God — the accumulated perversion of the human heart that God marks, bears, forgives, and will ultimately remember no more

record.context_description: Term names guilt as the inner-being weight of iniquity — the moral stain that accumulates, is confessed and borne, and can be removed by God alone

operations.description: New group 2437-001 for H5771H: Term names guilt as the inner-being weight of iniquity — the moral stain that accumulates, is confessed and borne, and can be removed by God alone

record.notes: physical/ornamental simile; no inner-being engagement
